channel.title: meowdioce — writing
channel.description: Long-form essays and lab notes on reverse engineering, Windows internals, anti-cheat infrastructure, browser sandbox internals, and the strange behavior of large language models.

item.title: Devirtualizing VMProtect 3.x without a VM.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: A field guide — dispatch graph, three known handlers, and patience for fixed points.

item.title: ETW providers as the new strace.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: Every Microsoft-Windows-* provider worth subscribing to in 2026, ranked by signal density.

item.title: Patchguard is not your friend.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: Why "Driver Verifier off, PatchGuard ignored" is the new normal in research VMs.

item.title: Activation steering as cheap interpretability.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: Two evenings, a 7B model, and a steering vector that catches when the model is bluffing about citations.

item.title: When the JIT becomes a weapon.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: V8 Maglev's bug class — five recent type-confusion patterns and why they keep coming back.

item.title: Anti-cheat is a UX problem.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: Detection is solved; experience is not. A short manifesto on what sits between a true positive and a justified ban.

item.title: Reading transformers like assembly.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: What you can learn from staring at attention patterns the way you'd stare at a disassembly window.

item.title: DMA cards, three years later.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: The state of hardware cheat hardware in 2025 — what works, what got expensive, what got banned.

item.title: Notes from a year inside Hyper-V.
item.dc:creator: meowdioce
item.description: Why nested virtualization is the most under-documented platform in the Windows research stack.
